title: Isaacman defends NASA budget proposal despite steep cuts
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman defended a proposed 25% budget cut for fiscal year 2027, despite significant reductions to the agency's funding. The proposal has sparked debate within the space industry.

title: NASA stops work on SLS Mobile Launcher 2
summary: NASA has halted construction on the second Mobile Launcher (ML-2) for the Space Launch System (SLS), as the upgraded version it was intended for is no longer in development.

title: NASA finalizes science plans for Artemis 2 lunar flyby
summary: NASA is finalizing science plans for the Artemis 2 lunar flyby mission. Astronauts will conduct observations during their lunar flyby.

title: Atlas 5 launches Amazon Leo satellites
summary: An Atlas 5 rocket successfully launched a new batch of satellites for Amazon's broadband constellation on April 4. The mission aims to speed up the deployment of Amazon's satellite network.

title: Space Force budget would more than double in Trump’s $1.5 trillion defense plan
summary: The U.S. Space Force's budget would more than double to $71 billion in fiscal year 2027 under Trump's proposed $1.5 trillion defense plan, with over $60 billion allocated for procurement, research, and development.

title: Artemis 2 in good shape cruising towards the moon
summary: The Artemis 2 Orion spacecraft is performing well with only minor issues after its engine burn toward the moon. The mission remains on track after its recent engine ignition.

title: Italy’s Argotec plans to scale Florida satellite facility to meet rising US demand
summary: Argotec opened its first U.S. satellite production facility near Kennedy Space Center in Florida to meet rising demand from American space programs. The expansion underscores Italy's growing presence in the U.S. space industry.

title: White House again proposes steep NASA budget cuts
summary: The White House has proposed significant budget cuts for NASA for the second year in a row, which would heavily impact the agency's science programs and the International Space Station.

title: SpaceX and Amazon spar over satellite deployments
summary: Amazon plans to revise its broadband satellite constellation deployment plans following criticism from SpaceX, which claims the current approach poses a space safety risk.

title: Europe’s strategic autonomy in space will define its role in the ‘second space age’
summary: Europe's future in space hinges on achieving strategic autonomy to avoid reliance on foreign technology in the 'second space age'. The European Union is prioritizing independent access, control, and development in space to secure its role.

title: Humanity is back at the moon! Artemis 2 astronauts arrive in lunar space
summary: The four astronauts of NASA's Artemis 2 mission have entered the lunar sphere of influence, marking humanity's return to the moon. This milestone brings them closer to the planned lunar flyby mission.

title: When the moon hits your eye from your Orion ship up high, that's a 'mare' for Artemis 2
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts observed the moon from their Orion spacecraft, expressing excitement about the experience. The event highlights the mission's progress toward lunar exploration.

title: Artemis 2 astronauts are about to see one of the rarest skywatching sights of all — a solar eclipse from beyond the moon
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts will observe a rare solar eclipse from beyond the moon's far side on April 6, using the event for scientific observations.

title: The breakout star of NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission isn't an astronaut — it's the space toilet
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission features a space toilet as a notable innovation, enabling astronauts to use the facilities during their lunar journey. The toilet system has been a point of interest due to its critical role in long-duration space missions.

title: The powerful new Rubin Observatory just found 11,000 new asteroids and measured 'tens of thousands more'
summary: The Rubin Observatory has discovered 11,000 new asteroids and measured tens of thousands more, offering a preview of the expected discovery surge once full operations begin.

title: Artemis 2 moon astronauts will try to recreate Apollo 8's historic 'Earthrise' photo during April 6 flyby
summary: The Artemis 2 crew plans to recreate Apollo 8's iconic 'Earthrise' photo during an April 6 flyby of the Moon. This mission aims to honor the historic 1968 image while advancing NASA's lunar exploration goals.

title: This is the inspiring Easter message an Artemis 2 astronaut has for Earth while traveling to the moon (video)
summary: NASA astronaut Victor Glover delivered an impromptu Easter message during the Artemis 2 mission as the Orion capsule traveled toward the moon. The message was shared while the crew was en route to Earth's natural satellite.

title: Dozens of hidden star streams found in the outskirts of our Milky Way galaxy
summary: Astronomers discovered dozens of hidden stellar streams in the outskirts of the Milky Way using Gaia data, providing new insights into galaxy formation and dark matter.

title: Lego's educational space sets are a stellar way to teach kids about the basic physics of collisions and impacts
summary: Lego's educational space-themed sets are highlighted as an effective tool for teaching children about physics concepts like collisions and impacts. The article humorously emphasizes the importance of engaging kids in STEM learning.

title: Artemis 2 astronauts — now halfway to the moon — report 'burning smell' from toilet, but everything's fine
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts reported a burning smell from their Orion spacecraft toilet, but Mission Control is not overly concerned. They are now over halfway to the moon.

title: Thinking of You, Earth
summary: NASA's Artemis II crew, including astronaut Reid Wiseman, will travel towards the Moon in 2026, with Wiseman observing Earth from the Orion spacecraft's window. The mission includes NASA astronauts Christina Koch and Victor Glover, as well as CSA astronaut.

title: NASA Answers Your Most Pressing Artemis II Questions
summary: NASA updated details about the Artemis II mission, the first crewed test flight under the Artemis program, including corrected timing for lunar flyby activities. The mission involves four astronauts aboard the Orion spacecraft flying around the Moon.

title: Illuminated in Orion
summary: NASA astronaut Christina Koch and CSA astronaut Jeremy Hansen are seen in the Orion crew capsule, with lights turned off to avoid glare. The image was taken in April 2026 during preparations for a mission.

title: NASA Sets Coverage for Northrop Grumman’s CRS-24 Resupply Launch
summary: NASA, Northrop Grumman, and SpaceX are targeting April 8 for the CRS-24 resupply launch to the International Space Station. The mission will deliver 11,000 pounds of cargo using Northrop Grumman's Cygnus XL spacecraft aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket.

title: Meet NASA’s New Artemis II Science Officers
summary: NASA has appointed Kelsey Young, Trevor Graff, and Angela Garcia as the first Artemis II science officers, integrating advanced lunar science into Mission Control. Their roles will enhance future Artemis crews' ability to observe and analyze the Moon.

title: Virgil I. “Gus” Grissom
summary: The news commemorates the 100th anniversary of Virgil I. 'Gus' Grissom's birth, highlighting his role as one of NASA's first seven astronauts and the second American in space aboard Liberty Bell 7 in 1961.

title: Hello, World
summary: NASA astronaut Reid Wiseman, commander of the Artemis II mission, captured a stunning image of Earth from the Orion spacecraft, showcasing two auroras and zodiacal light. This photo, along with another of Earth, marks the first images taken after the translunar injection burn.

title: NASA’s Artemis II Mission Leaves Earth Orbit for Flight around Moon
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission has successfully completed a translunar injection burn, marking the first time astronauts will fly around the Moon in over 50 years. The six-minute engine firing propelled the Orion spacecraft out of Earth's orbit toward the Moon.

title: Artemis II Astronauts Launch to Moon
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission will launch four astronauts on a 10-day journey around the Moon and back in April 2026. The mission will be conducted using NASA's Space Launch System rocket and Orion spacecraft.

title: Orion Spacecraft Races Toward Historic Lunar Flyby in Artemis II Mission
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is in its critical mid-flight phase as the Orion spacecraft approaches a historic lunar flyby. This marks a significant milestone for the mission carrying the first crewed lunar flyby since Apollo 17.

title: Artemis II Orion performs TLI Burn — Now headed to the Moon
summary: NASA's Orion spacecraft successfully performed the Trans-Lunar Injection (TLI) burn, marking a historic milestone as it heads toward the Moon. This achievement is part of the Artemis II mission, the first crewed lunar mission in over 50 years.

title: Artemis II returns humanity to the Moon with launch on Wednesday evening
summary: The Artemis II mission marks humanity's return to the Moon with a scheduled launch on Wednesday evening. This follows the historic Apollo 17 mission, which concluded in 1972.

title: Launch Preview: SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz launches comprise busy launch manifest
summary: Nine launches are scheduled this week, including SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz rockets from Florida, California, and Russia.

title: America’s Rocket Factory comes Full Circle with Artemis
summary: NASA's Artemis program marks a significant milestone as America's rocket industry completes a full circle in preparation for crewed missions beyond low Earth orbit. The development highlights the culmination of efforts to revive deep-space human spaceflight capabilities.

title: SpaceX delivers 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit on Transporter 16
summary: SpaceX successfully launched 119 payloads to a Sun-synchronous orbit as part of the Transporter 16 mission from California. This marks another milestone in SpaceX's rideshare program.

title: NASA outlines Moon Base plans, pivots on Gateway
summary: NASA announced detailed plans for a Moon Base during its 'Ignition' event, marking a significant shift in its lunar exploration strategy. The agency also indicated a pivot away from the Lunar Gateway project.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket
summary: Isar Aerospace, a European private space launch provider based in Germany, has scrubbed the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket.

title: NASA unveils Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars in 2028
summary: NASA announced the Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars, planned for launch in 2028. Administrator Jared Isaacman and other agency leaders revealed the details of this significant initiative.

title: Progress on Starbase Pads ahead of Block 3 Starships
summary: SpaceX is accelerating ground activities at Starbase to prepare for the upcoming Block 3 Starships, aiming to increase launch cadence. The progress highlights the company's focus on rapid iteration and infrastructure upgrades.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified space industry topic. No specific details were provided in the given news snippet.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of the European Space Agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A new silicon chip has been developed that integrates multiple functions, potentially revolutionizing space technology. This advancement could impact various sectors within the space industry.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long anomaly caused a loss of communication.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of CM25, the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025, focusing on industrial opportunities and program priorities. The events aim to inform and engage the industrial community on planned activities and thematic priorities.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to hunt for Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator chamber. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is scheduled for launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA will release ECOS version 6 on March 30, 2026, requiring all suppliers to use it for financial proposals in ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher has developed a compact, 3D-printed lens antenna for satellite navigation that is resistant to interference and cost-effective to produce. The football-sized antenna offers a lightweight alternative to bulky and expensive existing solutions.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly onboard Proba-3's Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communication.

title: White House Requests $18.8B FY27 Budget for NASA
summary: The White House has proposed a $18.8 billion budget for NASA in fiscal year 2027, matching the previous year's request but including cuts to science and STEM missions.

title: Insurance is Commercial Space Nuclear’s Biggest Headache
summary: Insurance is identified as the biggest challenge for commercial space nuclear missions, with experts calling it a 'show stopper' during an industry seminar. The post highlights the critical hurdle of securing insurance coverage for such ventures.

title: Charts Defining the Space Industry in Q1 2026
summary: The article discusses key trends and statistics defining the space industry in Q1 2026, with a focus on the resurgence of Earth Observation (EO) satellites.

title: CLPS Companies Excited For NASA’s ‘Opportunity Bomb’ Lunar Plan
summary: NASA announced a plan for a lunar base supplied through monthly uncrewed landings starting next year, sparking excitement among CLPS companies. The initiative, dubbed an 'opportunity bomb,' aims to accelerate lunar exploration and commercial opportunities.

title: Phantom Space Acquires Thermal Management Technologies
summary: Phantom Space has acquired Thermal Management Technologies, a company specializing in advanced satellite thermal components for in-space applications.

title: Artemis II Is On The Way To The Moon
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is en route to the Moon with a crew including the first woman and person of color to head to the Moon, as well as the first non-American astronaut. The mission marks a historic step in space exploration.

title: Exclusive: In-Orbit Manufacturing Startup Dispatch Emerges from Stealth
summary: Dispatch, an in-orbit manufacturing startup, has emerged from stealth mode with $500,000 in funding to develop an uncrewed space station for hosting manufacturing infrastructure.

title: Antaris Raises $28M Series A
summary: Antaris has raised $28 million in a Series A funding round led by WestWave Capital, with participation from Lockheed Martin Ventures and other investors. This investment will support the company's growth in the space industry.

title: Top Space Lawmaker on Moon Base, Artemis Plans
summary: A top space lawmaker expressed strong support for establishing a Moon base and sees it as a potential launch point for Mars missions. The remarks highlight growing enthusiasm among colleagues for the Artemis program's goals.

title: Voyager and Icarus Team Up on ISS Robotics Demo
summary: Voyager and Icarus are collaborating on a robotics demonstration aboard the International Space Station (ISS), marking a significant step in space-based automation. This partnership highlights the growing role of private companies in advancing ISS operations and future space missions.

title: Live in the Booth: Matt Desch Talks Iridium NTN Direct and Alt PNT Advances
summary: Iridium is launching Iridium NTN Direct, a standardized and global narrow-band satellite IoT service, as announced by CEO Matt Desch. The company is also advancing alternative PNT (Positioning, Navigation, and Timing) solutions.

title: Redwire Wins European Quantum Satellite Contract
summary: Redwire has won a contract to develop a quantum-secure satellite for Europe under the ESA's Quantum Key Distribution Satellite (QKDSat) program.

title: African Market for Satellite Services Offers Pent Up Demand
summary: Africa's large landmass has posed challenges for satellite services, but demand is growing as of September 2025.

title: Amazon in Talks to Buy Globalstar, According to FT Report
summary: Amazon is reportedly in discussions to acquire Globalstar, according to a Financial Times report. The potential deal was first reported on Wednesday evening.

title: Gravitics Receives Strategic Funding Increase From SpaceWERX
summary: Gravitics secured a Strategic Funding Increase (STRATFI) contract from SpaceWERX to accelerate the development of its Orbital Carrier architecture. This funding aims to support the U.S. Space Force's innovation initiatives.

title: Astroscale and Exotrail to Co-Develop Servicing Mission
summary: Astroscale and Exotrail will collaborate on a satellite de-orbiting mission, combining Japanese and French expertise in on-orbit servicing. The partnership aims to develop a capability for safely removing satellites from orbit.

title: In-Orbit Logistics Companies Call for Clearer Demand Signals from Gov
summary: In-orbit logistics companies are urging the government to provide clearer demand signals. The Space Development Agency highlights the challenges of operating in space during SATShow Week.

title: Canada’s Northwestel to Provide Broadband With Telesat Lightspeed Capacity
summary: Canada’s Northwestel has signed a multi-year contract with Telesat to use capacity from its Lightspeed constellation for broadband services. The agreement will support broadband provision in Canada’s northern regions, including Nunavut and the Northwest Territories.

title: Phantom Space Acquires Component Supplier Thermal Management Technologies
summary: Phantom Space Corporation has acquired Thermal Management Technologies (TMT) to support its orbital data center constellation development and vertical integration strategy. The acquisition aims to accelerate the company's plans in the space industry.

title: ArkEdge Space to Work With ABIT For IoT Service Rollout
summary: ArkEdge Space and ABIT Corporation have signed an MoU to jointly develop IoT solutions and satellite communication devices. The partnership aims to enhance satellite-based IoT services.
